rejected_findings.finding: Settlement queue flush() race condition (AUDIT-005) — non-atomic boolean flushing flag
rejected_findings.reason: False positive for single-threaded Node.js. The check `if (flushing)` and assignment `flushing = true` execute synchronously with no await between them. No code can interleave in the Node.js single-threaded event loop. Only applicable to Worker threads, not standard usage.

rejected_findings.finding: Settlement entry IDs use only 16 bytes (128 bits) — birthday paradox collision risk (AUDIT-004)
rejected_findings.reason: 128-bit randomness (UUID v4 standard) is sufficient. 2^64 operations required for birthday collision — impossible at any practical throughput. Over-engineering.

notes.note: This is a TypeScript library (no UI/web frontend) — UX_AUDIT, SEO_AUDIT, ACCESSIBILITY_AUDIT, MONETIZATION_AUDIT are all irrelevant.
npm publish is always a human task for this project — requires @te-btc scope on npm. Do not create an autonomous worker task for it.
Docker/aesir integration tests (real mint) are infrastructure tasks requiring human setup — cannot be created as fully autonomous code worker tasks.
Phase 5 items (NUT-15, NIP-98, third-party caveats, delegation chains, token refresh, metrics export) are explicitly out of scope until Phase 3 and 4 are complete.

verifyCashuPaymentSmart does NOT fall back for P2PK proofs with DLEQ failure — this is intentional (a DLEQ failure = something wrong, not a fallback case). The fallback path is only for non-P2PK-locked proofs.

feature_audit_raw.json (PRAS run from 2026-02-28) is stale — all 'unverified' warnings for ROADMAP items are false positives. The features were implemented in tasks 001-013 (177→265 tests). Do not create tasks from feature_audit_raw.json issues.
